name: pwm_controller
description: Parameterized PWM controller with synchronous counter-based duty generation. The design counts on clk when enable is asserted, wraps to zero at period, and drives pwm_out high while the current count is less than duty_cycle. All registers clear to zero when reset_n is low.

fixed_assumptions: Counter and output logic are fully synchronous to clk with active-low reset_n.
period and duty_cycle are sampled as live inputs each cycle; no shadow register behavior is assumed.

functionality: Implements an 8-bit parameterized PWM generator. On each rising edge of clk, if reset_n is deasserted low, counter_value and pwm_out are synchronously cleared to zero. When reset_n is high and enable is asserted, the internal counter increments by one every clock. If the counter reaches the programmed period value, it wraps to zero on the next enabled clock cycle. The pwm_out signal is asserted when counter_value is strictly less than duty_cycle, providing a pulse-width modulated output whose duty depends on the programmed threshold. The module continuously exposes the current counter_value on its output port.
responsibilities: Maintain a synchronous 8-bit counter with enable gating.
Implement wrap-to-zero behavior at the configured period boundary.
Generate pwm_out as a combinational comparison of counter_value against duty_cycle, registered through the synchronous update flow implied by the counter state.
Ensure all state returns to zero under active-low reset_n.
Preserve synthesizable RTL suitable for standard ASIC synthesis and STA flows.
reset_behavior: When reset_n is low, all sequential state is forced to zero. Upon release of reset_n, counter_value resumes from zero and pwm_out remains low until counter_value is less than duty_cycle according to normal operation.
behavior_rules: counter_value increments by 1 on each rising edge of clk only when enable is high and reset_n is high.
If the enabled counter state reaches the programmed period value, the next enabled state wraps to 0 rather than continuing to increment indefinitely.
If enable is low, counter_value holds its current value.
pwm_out is high exactly when the current counter_value is less than duty_cycle; otherwise it is low.
The design must be synthesizable with no inferred latches, no tri-states, and no unsized arithmetic.
All input widths are fixed at 1-bit control or 8-bit data per the spec.
period and duty_cycle are treated as 8-bit unsigned values.
counter_value is an 8-bit unsigned representation of the current counter state.
If duty_cycle is zero, pwm_out remains low for all counter states.
If duty_cycle is greater than the current counter_value, pwm_out is high; otherwise low.
